mission.title: Install and Configure Kcl on Kubernetes
mission.description: KCL is an open-source, constraint-based record and functional programming language designed to enhance the writing of complex configurations, particularly in cloud-native scenarios. Installing KCL allows you to generate and manage configuration data more effectively, reducing boilerplate and improving modularity.

steps.title: Add the KCL Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official KCL Helm repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add kcl-lang https://charts.kcl-lang.io --namespace kcl --create-namespace
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KCL using Helm
steps.description:
Install KCL in your Kubernetes cluster using the Helm chart.
```bash
helm install kcl kcl-lang/kcl --namespace kcl --create-namespace --version 0.11.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the KCL pods are running successfully in the kcl namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kcl
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the KCL deployment to set resource limits for better performance.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kcl --namespace kcl
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     memory: "512Mi"
#     cpu: "500m"
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the KCL service to access it from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/kcl --namespace kcl 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KCL will show the KCL pods running in the kcl namespace when you execute `kubectl get pods --namespace kcl`. You should also be able to access the KCL service via `http://localhost:8080` after setting up port forwarding.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kcl-lang https://charts.kcl-lang.io --namespace kcl --create-namespace
helm repo update

kubectl edit deployment kcl --namespace kcl
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     memory: "512Mi"
#     cpu: "500m"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
